title: ZooKeeper Request Latency P95 and P99 from mntr
spl:
index=infrastructure sourcetype="zookeeper:mntr"
| eval avg_lat=tonumber(zk_avg_latency)
| eval max_lat=tonumber(zk_max_latency)
| bin _time span=5m
| stats perc95(avg_lat) as p95_avg perc99(max_lat) as p99_max by host, _time
| where p95_avg > 50 OR p99_max > 200
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable: this use case is not built on a CIM data model. Rely on the search in Step 2 against your raw logs or metrics.
description: Average and max latency percentiles summarize client-visible slowness better than single snapshots. Rising p95 with stable load often precedes capacity limits on CPU or txn log devices.
value: Supports SLO dashboards for platform teams owning shared ZooKeeper clusters.
grandmaExplanation: We use this to support service goals dashboards for platform teams owning shared ZooKeeper clusters.
implementation: Collect dense enough samples for meaningful percentiles (≤10s). Do not enable `mntr` from untrusted networks. Store per-ensemble index. Compare across rolling upgrades.
knownFalsePositives: Response time spikes during JVM garbage collection, connection pool exhaustion, or backend dependency degradation. Load tests, campaigns, and cold caches also move percentiles.
